pom.xml.general_idea: Replace javax.annotation-api with jakarta.annotation-api to align with Jakarta EE namespace changes in Spring Boot 3.
pom.xml.why_exists: javax.annotation-api is removed or incompatible with Jakarta EE 9+ namespaces required by Spring Boot 3; compilation fails if code imports javax.annotation.*

registry-core-elasticsearch/pom.xml.general_idea: Migrate the annotation API dependency from the legacy javax namespace to the Jakarta namespace required by Spring Boot 3 and Java 17+ ecosystems.
registry-core-elasticsearch/pom.xml.why_exists: The javax.annotation package is no longer included in the JDK or standard Spring Boot 3 dependencies; using it causes ClassNotFoundException at runtime or compilation errors if the old artifact is removed from the parent BOM.

registry-core-helper/pom.xml.general_idea: Add Jakarta Annotation API dependency to replace removed javax.annotation module in Java 17.
registry-core-helper/pom.xml.why_exists: javax.annotation package was removed from the JDK in Java 11 and fully absent in Java 17; code using @Generated or other javax annotations will fail to compile without the Jakarta replacement.

registry-core/pom.xml.general_idea: Migrate annotation dependency from javax to jakarta namespace to comply with Jakarta EE 9+ standards required by Spring Boot 3.
registry-core/pom.xml.why_exists: Spring Boot 3 and Java 17+ environments use the jakarta.* namespace; using javax.annotation will result in ClassNotFoundException or NoClassDefFoundError at runtime because the classes are no longer present under the javax prefix.
